title: 11 Herbal Creams to Ease Inflamed Gums for Smokers

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams are a natural remedy that can help soothe inflamed gums. The creams contain triterpenoid saponins and carotenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These compounds help reduce swelling, fight off infection, and promote wound healing. When applied to inflamed gums, Calendula officinalis creams can ease discomfort, pain, and inflammation.  To use Calendula officinalis creams for inflamed gums, apply a thin layer to the affected area with a clean finger or cotton swab. Gently massage the cream into the gums for a few seconds. Repeat the application 2-3 times a day, ideally after brushing and flossing teeth. For best results, use the creams in conjunction with good oral hygiene practices, such as regular dental check-ups and a balanced diet. When you start using Calendula officinalis creams, you may notice a reduction in gum inflammation and swelling within a few days. The pain and discomfort associated with inflamed gums may also decrease. As the cream continues to work, you may see an improvement in the overall health of your gums, which can help prevent further problems. However, it's essential to note that Calendula officinalis creams are not a replacement for medical treatment. If you have persistent or severe gum inflammation, consult your dentist for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for inflamed gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams are a natural remedy for inflamed gums. They contain properties like aloe-emodin, aloe-bergenin, and aloe-aloenin that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. These constituents help soothe and calm inflamed gums, reducing redness and swelling. Aloe barbadensis creams also contain vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and potassium, which promote healing and tissue repair. To use aloe barbadensis cream for inflamed gums, gently apply a small amount to the affected area with a clean finger or a cotton swab. You can do this 2-3 times a day, ideally after brushing your teeth and before meals. Be sure to rinse your mouth with water after applying the cream to avoid any residue. You may also want to consider combining this treatment with good oral hygiene practices, such as regular brushing and flossing, to help prevent further inflammation. As you use aloe barbadensis cream, you may notice a reduction in inflammation and pain within a few days. Your gums may start to feel softer and less tender, and the redness may decrease. With consistent use, you may be able to prevent further inflammation and promote a healthy, pink appearance to your gums.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for inflamed gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams can help with inflamed gums due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which come from compounds like alkylamides, caffeic acid, and rosmarinic acid. These constituents work by reducing swelling and fighting off infection-causing bacteria. When applied topically to inflamed gums, Echinacea purpurea creams can help to soothe and calm the area, reducing pain and discomfort. To use Echinacea purpurea creams for inflamed gums, gently apply a thin layer to the affected area using a clean finger or cotton swab. You can do this 2-3 times a day, ideally after brushing your teeth and rinsing your mouth. As the cream works to reduce inflammation and promote healing, you may start to notice a decrease in pain and swelling within a few days. Your gums may also appear less red and inflamed, and any bleeding may slow down or stop altogether.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for inflamed gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams, also known as tea tree oil creams, can be a helpful remedy for inflamed gums due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream contains a compound called caryophyllene, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and fight off bacteria that can cause gum infections. The tea tree oil in the cream also contains a compound called terpinen-4-ol, which has been proven to have antimicrobial properties that can help combat the bacteria that can cause gum inflammation. To use Melaleuca alternifolia creams for inflamed gums, gently apply a small amount to the affected area using a cotton swab. You can do this 2-3 times a day, or as needed. Make sure to follow the instructions on the packaging and consult with a dentist or healthcare professional before using any new products on your gums. When you apply the cream, you may notice a reduction in swelling and redness in your gums within a few days. The cream may also help to reduce the pain and discomfort associated with gum inflammation. However, it's essential to note that Melaleuca alternifolia creams are not a replacement for regular dental care, and you should continue to brush and floss your teeth as usual. With regular use, you can help to soothe and calm your inflamed gums, and prevent future infections.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for inflamed gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis creams have been used for centuries to help soothe inflamed gums. This is because the herb, also known as sage, contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. These properties help reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums. Additionally, sage has antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial infections that can cause gum inflammation. To use Salvia officinalis creams for inflamed gums, first rinse your mouth with warm salt water to remove any debris. Then, apply a small amount of the cream directly to the affected area using a cotton swab. Gently massage the cream into your gums for about 30 seconds. Repeat this process 2-3 times a day. You can also use a mouthwash or rinse with sage tea to further help reduce inflammation. When you use Salvia officinalis creams, you may notice a reduction in swelling and pain within a few days. Your gums may start to feel smoother and less inflamed. In some cases, you may even notice a decrease in bad breath or an improvement in the overall health of your gums. However, if your symptoms persist or worsen, you should consult a dentist or healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal creams for inflamed gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams can be a helpful treatment for inflamed gums. The cream contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which are compounds that can help reduce swelling and inflammation. The flavonoids, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, have antioxidant properties that can help protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals. The terpenoids, including bilobalide and ginkgolides, can help improve blood flow to the gums, which can aid in healing and reducing pain. If you're looking to try Ginkgo biloba cream for inflamed gums, start by applying a small amount to the affected area 2-3 times a day. Gently massage the cream into the gums using your finger, making sure to cover the entire area. You can also swish the cream around in your mouth to help it penetrate deeper into the gums. Be sure to spit out any excess cream and not swallow it, as it's not meant for internal use. It may take some time to see results, but you can expect to notice a reduction in swelling and inflammation within a few days to a week. As you continue to use the Ginkgo biloba cream, you may notice that your gums feel softer and less painful. The inflammation may decrease, and you may be able to eat and speak more comfortably. In some cases, you may even notice an improvement in the color and texture of your gums. However, if your symptoms persist or worsen, it's best to consult with your dentist or healthcare provider for further guidance and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for inflamed gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Artemisia absinthium creams have been traditionally used to help with inflamed gums. This is because the plant contains compounds like thujone, camphor, and borneol, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. These properties help to reduce swelling and fight off infection in the gums. When applied topically, the cream can also help to soothe and calm the gums, reducing pain and discomfort. To use Artemisia absinthium cream for inflamed gums, first, make sure to clean and dry the affected area. Then, apply a small amount of cream to the affected gum using a cotton swab. Gently massage the cream into the gum, making sure it's fully absorbed. Repeat this process 2-3 times a day, as needed. It's also a good idea to consult with a healthcare professional or dentist before using any new treatment, especially if you have a pre-existing condition or are taking any medications. As you use the cream, you may start to notice a decrease in inflammation and swelling in your gums. The pain and discomfort may also start to subside. You may also notice that your gums are healing faster and are less sensitive. However, it's essential to continue using the cream as directed and to maintain good oral hygiene habits, such as brushing and flossing regularly, to prevent further inflammation and gum disease.
remedies.image_alt: Artemisia absinthium herbal creams for inflamed gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams are a popular remedy for soothing inflamed gums. The key to their effectiveness lies in their active compounds, such as eucalyptol and camphor, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These constituents help reduce swelling and fight off bacteria that cause infections. When used topically, eucalyptus globulus creams can penetrate deep into the gum tissue, providing quick relief from pain and discomfort. To use eucalyptus globulus creams for inflamed gums, gently apply a thin layer to the affected area using a cotton swab or your finger. Avoid swallowing or ingesting the cream, as it can be toxic if taken internally. For best results, use the cream 2-3 times a day, or as directed by your dentist or pharmacist. You may also want to rinse your mouth with warm salt water before applying the cream to help loosen any debris or bacteria. As the cream takes effect, you can expect to experience a reduction in swelling and pain. The inflammation should subside within a few days, and you may notice an improvement in the overall health of your gums. However, if your symptoms persist or worsen, it's essential to consult with your dentist or healthcare provider to rule out any underlying conditions that may require professional attention. Regular use of eucalyptus globulus creams can also help prevent future episodes of inflamed gums by promoting healthy gum tissue and reducing the risk of infection.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for inflamed gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams, also known as turmeric creams, have been shown to help with inflamed gums due to their active ingredient, curcumin. Curcumin has powerful anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce swelling and fight off infection in the gums. It also contains other beneficial compounds like vitamin C, which supports collagen production and wound healing. To use Curcuma longa creams for inflamed gums, gently apply a small amount to the affected area using a clean finger. You can also mix a few drops of turmeric oil with your regular toothpaste for added benefits. Make sure to rinse your mouth thoroughly and spit out any excess paste.  When you start using Curcuma longa creams, you may notice a reduction in swelling and redness in your gums within a few days. The inflammation should decrease, and the gums may become less sore. In some cases, you may also experience an improvement in your breath and a reduction in bad tastes in your mouth. However, it's essential to note that Curcuma longa creams are not a substitute for regular dental care, so continue to brush and floss your teeth as usual.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for inflamed gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis creams have been used for centuries to soothe inflamed gums, and it's not hard to see why. The creams contain properties like tannins, flavonoids, and catechins, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. These constituents help reduce swelling, ease pain, and prevent infections in the gums. When you apply Camellia sinensis cream to your gums, the tannins help constrict blood vessels, reducing the flow of blood to the affected area and thereby reducing inflammation. The flavonoids, on the other hand, have antioxidant properties that help protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals. To use Camellia sinensis cream for inflamed gums, gently massage a small amount onto the affected area with your finger. Do this 2-3 times a day, and you may start to see improvements within a few days. Your gums should begin to feel softer and less painful, and any swelling should start to subside. As you continue to use the cream, you may also notice a decrease in the redness and a reduction in bad breath. Regular use may even help prevent future gum inflammation.
remedies.image_alt: Camellia sinensis herbal creams for inflamed gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams, also known as peppermint creams, can be helpful for inflamed gums due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The creams contain menthol, menthone, and limonene, which are known to reduce swelling and ease pain. The cooling sensation of menthol can also numb the area, providing temporary relief from discomfort.  To use Mentha x piperita creams for inflamed gums, apply a small amount to the affected area with a cotton swab. Gently massage it in, but avoid swallowing it. You can also apply it after brushing your teeth and before going to bed.  When applying the cream, you may notice a reduction in swelling and pain within a few minutes. The area may feel cooler and more comfortable. You may also notice a decrease in the redness and inflammation of your gums over time. However, if your symptoms persist or worsen, you should consult a dentist for proper evaluation and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for inflamed gums

intro_desc: Smokers often suffer from inflamed gums, also known as gum recession or gum disease. This happens because smoking reduces blood flow to the gums, making it harder for them to heal and fight off infection. As a result, smokers may experience pain, bleeding, and bad breath. Inflamed gums can also lead to tooth loss, which affects not only a person's self-esteem but also their ability to eat and speak properly. Smoking can also damage the bone that supports the teeth, making it harder to chew and digest food. This can lead to digestive problems, weight loss, and a lower quality of life. When inflamed gums go untreated, they can cause more serious health issues, such as heart disease and diabetes. Smokers who experience inflamed gums often struggle to find effective treatments, but one option is using herbal creams. These creams work by reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the gums. They contain natural ingredients that are absorbed quickly by the skin, providing relief from pain and discomfort. By applying these creams directly to the affected area, smokers can reduce their symptoms and promote healthy gums.
